item.title: Potential for anti‑angiogenic therapy targeting the receptor for advanced glycation end products/VEGF axis in ulcerative colitis (Review).
item.description: Molecular medicine reports - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a major form of inflammatory bowel disease, has a global incidence of ~10.6 per 100,000 individuals. The long‑term side effects and dependency issues associated with conven...

item.title: Highly Skewed Immunohistochemical αβ:γδ Ratios for T-Cell Receptors Can Be Detected in Reactive Duodenal Biopsies.
item.description: Archives of pathology & laboratory medicine - T-cell receptors (TCRs) are classified as αβ- and γδ-chain groups. T-cell lymphoma (TCL) has been shown to express either αβ or γδ TCRs. Immunohistochemical stains for TCR-BetaF1 (TCR-BF1) (αβ) and...

item.title: Risk factors for pentosan polysulfate maculopathy.
item.description: Canadian journal of ophthalmology. Journal canadien d'ophtalmologie - To identify risk factors for pentosan polysulfate (PPS) maculopathy. Cross-sectional study of PPS-exposed individuals seen at Emory Eye Center from January 2018 to March 2024. Data from standardize...

item.title: Subclinical atherosclerosis in inflammatory bowel disease: Prevalence, vascular distribution and implications for cardiovascular risk stratification.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Cardiovascular (CV) risk assessment in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains insufficiently explored. This study aimed to assess the role of vascular Doppler ultrasound in refining...

item.title: Is it justified to order an upfront cytomegalovirus immunohistochemical stain in patients with severe inflammatory bowel disease and ulceration?
item.description: Human pathology - Cytomegalovirus (CMV) infection is common amongst both immunocompetent and immunocompromised patients. Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are more prone to suffer from CMV complications...

item.title: Long-Term Risk of Inflammatory Bowel Disease With Sarcopenia Status: A Large-Scale Prospective Cohort Study.
item.description: Geriatrics & gerontology international - The relationship between sarcopenia and Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), remains poorly understood. We aimed to investigate the associat...

item.title: Therapeutic Impact on Quality of Life in Adult Patients With Chronic Intestinal Pseudo-obstruction: A Multicenter, Cohort Study.
item.description: Journal of neurogastroenterology and motility - : To evaluate the quality of life (QOL) of patients with chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction (CIPO) before and after treatment. : This study enrolled 50 adult patients diagnosed with CIPO at 4 in...

item.title: Large vessel vasculitis combined with inflammatory bowel disease: epidemiology, shared pathogenesis, and therapeutic advances.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - Concomitant existence of large vessel vasculitis (LVV) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is rare but has gained increasing clinical and research attention. LVV, comprising Takayasu arteritis (TA...
